Video Games
+ Science!
How the coolest games use real science 🧪🎮
→ Arrow keys to navigate →
We make games played by millions of people worldwide
Every one of these games is packed with real science. Let me show you!
🔬 Physics
The Science of Forces & Motion
Every jump, bounce, and explosion in a game uses real physics equations!
🧮
Math
Every pixel has X,Y coordinates. Moving = adding numbers 60 times/sec!
🍎
Gravity
Earth: 9.8 m/s². Moon: 1.6. Jupiter: 24.8. Games use real numbers!
💥
Collisions
When objects bounce, the computer calculates energy transfer — just like real billiards!
🔬 Physics — Try It!
Play With Physics!
Click to launch balls! Adjust gravity, bounciness, and friction.
🌍 Click to launch!
📐 Geometry
The Science of Shapes
Every 3D object in a game is built from tiny triangles!
🔺
Triangles
A character in VALORANT has ~20,000 triangles. Your GPU paints them all 60+ times/sec!
✨
More = Smoother
More triangles = smoother shapes. Minecraft uses few (blocky). Fortnite uses many (smooth).
💡
Lighting
The computer calculates how light bounces off each triangle to make flat shapes look round!
📐 Geometry — See It!
Everything Is Triangles!
Add more triangles and watch it get smoother. Change the rendering mode!
🔺 24 | Wireframe
🧠 Neurology
The Science of Your Brain + Color
Artists choose colors to hack your brain into feeling things!
😨
Dark = Danger
Deep blues and blacks trigger your brain's "be careful!" response. Horror games use these.
🌅
Warm = Energy
Oranges and reds activate excitement in your brain. Battle scenes and action zones use these!
🌿
Cool = Calm
Soft greens and light blues tell your nervous system "you're safe." Home bases use these.
🧠 Neurology — Feel It!
Same Scene, Different Mood!
Exact same shapes — only the colors changed. Look how different they feel!
🧪 Psychology
The Science of Behavior
Game designers use brain science to make games impossible to put down!
🎰
Random Rewards
Your brain gets MORE excited by surprises than guarantees. That's why loot drops feel amazing!
⏰
FOMO
"Limited time!" makes your brain panic. You play NOW because you're scared of missing out!
⚖️
Meaningful Choice
Sword or shield? Real tradeoffs make your brain engage deeper and care more!
🧪 Psychology — Prove It!
Fixed vs Random Rewards
Play both games. Same average coins. Which one feels more exciting?
Game A
🪙 0
 
Plays: 0
Game B
🪙 0
 
Plays: 0
🧪 The Science of Random Rewards
Both games average ~10 coins per play. But Game B feels way more exciting!

Your brain releases more dopamine from unexpected rewards than predictable ones. Scientists call this "prediction error" — the surprise is what feels good.

Game designers use this deliberately!
Click to close
📊 Sociology
The Science of People in Groups
Games track how millions of players behave — then use the data to improve!
🗺️
Heat Maps
Show where players die most. Red spot = something needs fixing!
📉
Tracking
Play time, battle pass opens, quit levels — millions of events daily.
🎚️
Tuning
Adjust loot rates, difficulty, prices — watch behavior change. A giant experiment!
📊 Sociology — Tune It!
Be the Data Scientist!
Adjust sliders → watch simulated players respond!
🧬 Biology
The Science of Evolution
We teach computers to play using evolution — survival of the fittest!
🧒
Try & Fail
Bots play thousands of times. At first they're TERRIBLE — crashing into everything!
🧬
Evolution
Best bots "have kids." Strategies get copied with random mutations — evolution in fast-forward!
🏆
Superhuman
AI has beaten world champions at Chess, Go, and StarCraft II!
🧬 Biology — Watch Evolution!
Survival of the Fittest
Bots steer like race cars through a maze. Hit a wall = dead. Best survivors' kids get smarter!
🧬 Gen: 1  |  🏆 0%  |  ✅ 0  |  😤 0/8
🧬 Settings
🔴 HoF 🟡 Elite 🟣 Bred
🟠 Explorer 🟢 Winner
🗣️ Linguistics
The Science of Language
AI learned to write by reading the entire internet — but it doesn't see words like you do!
📚
Read Everything
AI read billions of books, websites, and articles. Wikipedia, textbooks, stories, code — basically all the text humans have ever written.
🧩
Tokens
AI doesn't see whole words! It breaks text into chunks called tokens. "understanding" → "under" + "standing". "gameplay" → "game" + "play". It learned 50,000 of these chunks!
🔗
Patterns
From all that reading, it learned which tokens usually follow which. "Once upon a ___" → "time" almost always! That's all it knows — patterns.
🗣️ Linguistics — Tokens
AI Doesn't See Words Like You!
We read whole words. AI breaks everything into small chunks called tokens.
👀 How YOU read it:
The knight is understanding the gameplay
⬇️
🤖 How AI reads it:
Theknightisunderstandingthegameplay
7
Words you see
10
Tokens AI sees
50K
Total tokens AI knows
🗣️ How AI Writes
One Word at a Time
AI builds sentences by predicting the next word, adding it, then predicting again — in a loop!
1️⃣
Predict
Look at all the words so far. Calculate probability for every possible next token. "The brave ___" → knight 42%, warrior 20%, hero 18%...
2️⃣
Roll the Dice
It doesn't always pick the #1 word! It randomly rolls based on the probabilities. Higher chance = more likely, but surprises happen. That's where "creativity" comes from!
3️⃣
Loop
Add the picked word to the sentence. Go back to step 1. Repeat hundreds of times until the response is done. Every word you read from AI was picked this way!
🗣️ Linguistics — Watch AI Write!
One Word at a Time
Watch AI build a sentence — each click adds the next predicted word!
